reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.125 , reason:  The visited flag semantics are inverted compared to DFS (DFS uses 1 for visited, here 1 means unvisited). This inconsistency causes logical errors.

41.reason: This prints neighbors immediately when they are discovered/enqueued. In standard BFS, we print when we dequeue (visit) them. This causes incorrect ordering and duplicate prints if not handled carefully. Specifically, it prints the neighbor before it is processed as a node itself in the recursive step properly aligned with BFS levels. Standard BFS prints when dequeuing. Here it prints when enqueuing.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.125 , reason:  BFS should print vertices when they are dequeued, not immediately when enqueued. This changes the traversal order.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  This prints neighbors immediately when they are discovered/enqueued. In standard BFS, we print when we dequeue (visit) them. This causes incorrect ordering and duplicate prints if not handled carefully. Specifically, it prints the neighbor before it is processed as a node itself in the recursive step properly aligned with BFS levels. Standard BFS prints when dequeuing. Here it prints when enqueuing.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.2 , reason:  Printing immediately upon discovery is incorrect for BFS order; nodes should be printed when dequeued/processed.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  [BFS incorrectly prints adjacent nodes immediately without proper queue-based level order; also modifies jilu inconsistently]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [BFS incorrectly prints vertices immediately upon discovery without proper queue-based level order; also modifies jilu inconsistently]
